title.purpose: Social pill hero titles (Barq, Twitter, Facebook, Telegram).

label.purpose: The single 'find me' eyebrow — not reused elsewhere.

shadows.name: soft-ambient
shadows.purpose: Default resting elevation for chips and the bio card.

shadows.name: pop-ambient
shadows.purpose: Profile photo only — needs more contrast to lift off the busy backdrop.

shadows.name: accent-glow
shadows.purpose: Per-pill resting glow tinted by that pill's accent.

shadows.name: accent-glow-hover
shadows.purpose: Pill hover/focus state — glow intensifies and lifts.

motion.name: idle-bob
motion.purpose: Profile photo gentle float at rest.

motion.name: ring-spin
motion.purpose: Conic gradient ring rotation behind the pfp.

motion.name: glow-pulse
motion.purpose: Ambient glow breathing behind the pfp.

motion.name: bark-pop
motion.purpose: Tap feedback on the pfp — scale + rotate.

motion.name: letter-bounce
motion.purpose: Title letters bounce in a wave.

components.name: Social Pill (Hero)
components.description: 2-column grid social link button with accent-tinted background, icon, title, and handle.
components.css: .ds-pill-hero { width: 100%; min-height: 120px; padding: 16px 12px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; text-align: center; gap: 8px; border-radius: 16px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent) 28%, #fff) 0%, color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent) 12%, #fff) 100%); border: 2px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent) 55%, transparent); color: #2A2566; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 8px 20px color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent) 22%, transparent), 0 4px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); transition: transform 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease; } .ds-pill-icon { width: 38px; height: 38px; color: var(--accent); } .ds-pill-title { font-family: MNSeafood, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; letter-spacing: 1px; } .ds-pill-sub { font-family: naturaly, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; color: #4B4485; opacity: 0.8; margin-top: 3px; } .ds-pill-hero:hover, .ds-pill-hero:focus-visible { border-color: var(--accent); box-shadow: 0 14px 28px color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent) 35%, transparent), 0 4px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); transform: translateY(-3px); }

components.name: Chip
components.description: Small pastel pill tag used only for the 'about me' facts row.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-block; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.78); color: #2A2566; font-family: naturaly, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 999px; border: 1.5px solid rgba(139, 69, 19, 0.25); backdrop-filter: blur(4px); box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(106, 90, 205, 0.15); }

components.name: Bio Card
components.description: Frosted-glass card holding the bio paragraphs.
components.html: Hi, I'm Hachi 🐾 a fluffy little collie based in Thailand.
components.css: .ds-bio-card { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.55); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); border-radius: 20px; padding: 18px 20px; text-align: center; border: 1.5px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.6); box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(106, 90, 205, 0.15); color: #2A2566; } .ds-bio-card b { color: #8B4513; }

narrative.overview: This page reads like a sun-warmed postcard pinned to a corkboard: a pastel sky bleeding from blue into pink, sakura petals drifting past, a round-framed photo of a happy collie that wags when you tap it. It is warm and a little nostalgic rather than loud or toy-like — the kind of page a friend would send you, not a SaaS product would ship. Every surface is soft: frosted glass cards, rounded pill buttons, ambient glows instead of hard shadows. This system explicitly rejects the generic Linktree look: no flat stack of plain gray or white buttons, no interchangeable card-grid template, no corporate landing-page tropes (hero metrics, tiny uppercase eyebrows, gradient-text headlines). It also rejects toy-like 'kawaii pocket pet' bounciness as the dominant register — motion here is gentle (idle bob, slow spin, soft pulse), not springy or hyperactive. The page is built mobile-first: every button, chip, and tap target is sized for a thumb, not scaled down from a desktop layout.
keyCharacteristics: Pastel sky gradient (blue → pink) as the constant backdrop, never solid white or dark.
Frosted-glass ('paper') surfaces for every card and pill — translucency over flat fill.
Warm ink-purple text, never pure black or cool gray.
Each social pill carries its own brand accent color, set apart by ambient colored glow rather than a border stripe.
Motion is slow and breathing (4–12s loops), reserved for idle states; taps trigger quick, deliberate feedback (bark, pop, paw stamp), not bounce-everywhere choreography.

rules.name: The One Accent Per Pill Rule
rules.body: Each social pill owns exactly one brand accent color (--accent), expressed consistently as background tint, border, icon color, and glow. Accents never bleed into shared chrome.

rules.name: The Single Eyebrow Rule
rules.body: Only one section in the whole page ('find me') uses the small-caps tracked-label treatment. It is not a repeating scaffold.

rules.name: The No Hard Edge Rule
rules.body: Every shadow in this system is soft and color-tinted. A crisp, neutral-gray drop shadow is always wrong here.

dos: Do keep the pastel sky-to-petal gradient (#ADD8E6 → #C9E8F0 → #FFD1DC) as the constant page backdrop — never solid white or dark.
Do use frosted/translucent surfaces (paper, paper-strong) for every card, chip, and pill — opacity and blur, not flat fill.
Do give each social pill exactly one brand accent color, expressed consistently as background tint, border, icon, and glow (The One Accent Per Pill Rule).
Do keep shadows soft-edged and color-tinted (Soft Ambient / Accent Glow) — depth through glow, not hard drop shadows (The No Hard Edge Rule).
Do size every tap target for a thumb first; this page is read overwhelmingly on phones.
Do provide a prefers-reduced-motion fallback for every animation.
donts: Don't build a generic Linktree-style flat list of plain gray/white buttons.
Don't use a uniform card grid where every tile looks interchangeable.
Don't reach for corporate/SaaS landing-page tropes: no hero metrics, no gradient-clipped text, no tiny uppercase eyebrow above more than one section (The Single Eyebrow Rule).
Don't use a side-stripe border-left/border-right as a colored accent anywhere.
Don't use crisp neutral-gray drop shadows; every shadow here is soft and tinted (The No Hard Edge Rule).
Don't make ambient/idle motion fast or springy; reserve bounce/pop energy for direct tap feedback only.
